summary: Completed a repository-wide audit of Ralph/ralph/ralph-runner references and wrote an explicit rename-boundary inventory before the actual rebrand stories continue.
architectureNotes: This story intentionally keeps all edits inside .harness-runner governance artifacts so the rename inventory is reviewable without mixing with ongoing source-code migration work.
The runtime artifact root .harness-runner is already the architectural source of truth and must remain stable while symbolic and contribution-point names are renamed around it.
workspacePaths.ts is the primary reuse seam for later rename stories because it centralizes artifact locations; future refactors should rename helper symbols there first and fan out through imports.
The chat participant, command ids, configuration prefix, and package metadata form one contribution-point cluster and should be changed together to avoid VS Code manifest/runtime drift.
Documentation, localization, and tests form a second cluster that should move with runtime-visible naming changes so the reviewer can verify consistency from one surface to another.
keyDecisions: Capture the rebrand boundary as structured JSON under .harness-runner instead of scattering notes across transient chat context.
Preserve .harness-runner and existing Harness-branded paths as explicit do-not-rename rules.
Treat metadata.ralphLanguage as a compatibility seam to coordinate later rather than a blind rename target.
Avoid touching the dirty US-002 source-file changes already present in the worktree; keep US-001 isolated to auditable artifacts.
constraintsConfirmed: Git commit message must use Chinese.
No dist/ or out/ files were hand-edited.
At least one projectConstraints.testCommands command was executed before completion.
risks: The repository already contains interrupted US-002 worktree changes in package.json, src/extension.ts, src/localization.ts, src/projectConstraints.ts, and src/test/extension.test.ts. This story did not validate or amend them.

Generated/package outputs such as package-lock.json and the checked-in VSIX still carry old ralph-runner branding and will remain inconsistent until the metadata rename story is completed and packaging is regenerated.
Because this inventory is curated from search results, any newly introduced branding outside the scanned files will require a refresh later.
followUps: Execute US-002 against the contribution-point cluster: package metadata, activation ids, command ids, configuration prefixes, and chat participant ids/names.
Execute US-003 against TypeScript symbol and user-visible copy surfaces using the preserve rules recorded here.
After runtime rename stories land, regenerate Agent Map artifacts and refresh README wording to remove mixed RALPH/Harness branding.
Regenerate package-lock.json and package artifacts once package metadata is renamed.
